items.title: A folk musician became a target for AI fakes and a copyright troll
items.description:
Murphy Campbell is at the center of a brewing storm around AI and a broken copyright system. | Image: Murphy Campbell	

In January, folk artist Murphy Campbell discovered several songs on her Spotify profile that did not belong there. They were songs that she had recorded, but she'd never uploaded them to Spotify, and something was off about the vocals.
She quickly surmised that someone had pulled performances of the songs she posted to YouTube, created AI covers, and uploaded them to streaming platforms under her name. I ran one of the songs, "Four Marys", through two different AI detectors, and it seemed to support her suspicions with both saying it was probably AI-generated.

Read the full story at The Verge.
items.content: In January, folk artist Murphy Campbell discovered several songs on her Spotify profile that did not belong there. They were songs that she had recorded, but she’d never uploaded them to Spotify, and something was off about the vocals.She quickly surmised that someone had pulled performances of the songs she posted to YouTube, created AI covers, and uploaded them to streaming platforms under her name. I ran one of the songs, “Four Marys”, through two different AI detectors, and it seemed to support her suspicions with both saying it was probably AI-generated.Campbell was shocked, “I was kind of under the impression that we had a little bit more checks in place before someone could just do that. But, you know, a lesson learned there,” she told The Verge. It took some time before Campbell managed to get the fake songs removed, “I became a pest,” she said. And even then, it wasn’t a complete victory. While the offending tracks don’t appear to be available on YouTube Music or Apple Music anymore, at least one can still be found on Spotify, just under a different artist profile, but with the same name. There are now multiple Murphy Campbells — “Obviously, I was thrilled by that,” the real Murphy Campbell said.Spotify is testing a new system that would allow artists to manually approve songs before they appear on their profile, but Campbell is skeptical after being burned. “I feel like, every time, an entity that’s that large makes a promise like that to musicians. It seems to just not be what they made it out to be, but I’ll be curious to try it out in the future,” she said.This was just the beginning of Campbell’s nightmare, however.RelatedMusicians are getting really tired of this AI clone ‘bullshit’On the day that a Rolling Stone article was published, discussing Campbell’s brush with AI imitators, a series of videos were uploaded to YouTube through distributor Vydia. Those videos have not been posted publicly, and it’s unclear if anyone other than the uploader, who goes by Murphy Rider, has seen them. YouTube declined to comment for this story.Those were used to claim ownership of the material in several of Murphy Campbell’s videos. Campbell received a notice from YouTube reading: “You are now sharing revenues with the copyright owners of the music detected in your video, Darling Corey.” The most confusing part, the songs at the center of these claims are all in the public domain, including the classic “In the Pines,” which dates back to at least the 1870s and has been covered by everyone from Lead Belly to Nirvana (as “Where Did You Sleep Last Night”).Vydia has since released those claims, and spokesperson Roy LaManna says the person who uploaded the videos has been banned from their platform. Of the over 6,000,000 claims filed by Vydia through YouTube’s Content ID system, 0.02 percent were found to be invalid, which LaManna says is, “by industry standards is like amazing.” Continuing, “we pride ourselves on doing this the right way.”LaManna also says that Vydia has no connection to Timeless IR or the AI covers that were uploaded to streaming platforms under Campbell’s name. While the timing is certainly suspicious, LaManna says the two incidents are separate.Vydia has received a lot of blowback including, LaManna says, “literal death threats” which have led to the offices being evacuated. Campbell isn’t about to let Vydia off the hook, but notes that it’s not solely to blame. The worlds of generative AI, music distribution, and copyright are complex with multiple points of failure and opportunities for abuse. “I think it goes way deeper than we think it does,” Campbell says.
items.author: Terrence O’Brien
items.titleZh: 民间音乐家遭AI伪造与版权流氓盯上
items.descriptionZh:
今年1月，民间艺术家墨菲·坎贝尔（Murphy Campbell）在她的Spotify主页上发现了多首不属于自己的歌曲。这些虽是她曾演唱过的作品，却从未亲自上传至该平台，且人声部分存在明显异常。她迅速推断，有人从她发布于YouTube的表演视频中提取音频，通过AI生成仿唱版本，并冒用她的名义上传至流媒体平台。笔者将其一首歌曲《四玛丽》（Four Marys）交由两种不同的AI检测工具分析，结果均显示该曲目很可能由AI生成，印证了她的疑虑。

坎贝尔对此感到震惊：“我原以为平台至少会设置一些审核机制，没想到有人能如此轻易得手。这算是给我上了一课。”她向《The Verge》透露，经过一番周折才成功删除这些伪造歌曲，“我不得不像个讨嫌鬼一样反复投诉”。然而，这场维权并非彻底胜利——虽然涉事曲目已从YouTube Music和Apple Music下架，但至少仍有一首以相同歌名、不同艺术家主页的形式存留在Spotify上。“现在平台上冒出来好几个‘墨菲·坎贝尔’，简直荒唐。”她无奈表示。

尽管Spotify正在测试一项允许艺术家手动审核主页歌曲的新系统，但吃过亏的坎贝尔仍持怀疑态度：“每当这类大型平台向音乐人作出承诺时，结果往往不尽如人意。不过未来我还是会好奇试试看。”可她的噩梦并未就此结束。

在《滚石》杂志报道其遭遇AI仿冒事件的同一天，一批通过分销商Vydia上传的私人视频悄然出现在YouTube后台。这些由名为“墨菲·骑手”（Murphy Rider）的用户上传的视频虽未公开，却触发了YouTube的Content ID系统，导致坎贝尔收到通知：“您的视频《亲爱的科里》（Darling Corey）中检测到受版权保护的音乐，现已与版权方共享收益。”极具讽刺意味的是，被声索版权的歌曲均属公有领域作品，其中包含可追溯至19世纪70年代的经典民谣《在松林中》（In the Pines），该作品曾被从“铅肚皮”（Lead Belly）到涅槃乐队（Nirvana）等无数音乐人演绎。

Vydia随后撤销了这些不当声索，其发言人罗伊·拉曼纳（Roy LaManna）称上传者已被封禁，并强调平台通过Content ID系统提交的600万次版权声明中仅0.02%无效，“按行业标准已属卓越”。但拉曼纳坚称Vydia与涉事AI翻唱及背后公司Timeless IR无关，尽管时间点的巧合令人侧目。

此事引发激烈反弹，拉曼纳透露甚至收到死亡威胁，导致办公室被迫疏散。坎贝尔认为责任并非全在Vydia，生成式AI、音乐分发与版权体系交织成的复杂网络存在多重漏洞与滥用空间。“这潭水比我们想象得更深。”她指出，事件暴露出AI克隆技术滥用、平台审核机制薄弱、版权系统自动化缺陷等多重风险，尤其对独立艺术家的创作生态构成严峻挑战。在技术狂奔与法律滞后的夹缝中，如何构建兼顾创新与权益保护的治理框架，已成为数字音乐时代迫在眉睫的命题。

items.title: National Robotics Week — Latest Physical AI Research, Breakthroughs and Resources
items.description: This National Robotics Week, NVIDIA is highlighting the breakthroughs that are bringing AI into the physical world — as well as the growing wave of robots transforming industries, from agricultural and manufacturing to energy and beyond. Advancements in robot learning, simulation and foundation models are accelerating development, enabling robots to move from training in virtual […]
items.content: This National Robotics Week, NVIDIA is highlighting the breakthroughs that are bringing AI into the physical world — as well as the growing wave of robots transforming industries, from agricultural and manufacturing to energy and beyond. Advancements in robot learning, simulation and foundation models are accelerating development, enabling robots to move from training in virtual environments to real-world deployment faster than ever. With NVIDIA platforms for simulation, synthetic data and AI-powered robot learning, developers now have the tools to build machines that can perceive, reason and act in complex environments. Check back here all week for coverage on the latest NVIDIA physical AI technologies.
items.author: NVIDIA Writers
items.titleZh: 全国机器人周——最新实体人工智能研究、突破与资源
items.descriptionZh:
值此国家机器人周之际，英伟达重点展示了将人工智能带入物理世界的突破性进展，以及从农业、制造业到能源等领域日益增长的机器人转型浪潮。机器人学习、仿真和基础模型领域的进步正在加速发展，使得机器人能够比以往任何时候都更快地从虚拟环境训练转向现实世界部署。

借助英伟达在仿真、合成数据和AI驱动的机器人学习方面的平台，开发者如今拥有了构建能够在复杂环境中感知、推理和行动的机器所需的工具。这些技术进步正在深刻改变多个行业：在农业领域，配备了先进视觉系统的机器人可以精准识别并清除杂草，减少除草剂的使用；在制造业，自主移动机器人能够灵活适应动态变化的生产线，提高物流效率；在能源行业，机器人可执行危险环境下的检测和维护任务，保障作业安全。

核心技术原理主要围绕三大支柱：一是通过Isaac Sim等高性能仿真平台，在虚拟世界中生成大量训练数据并安全测试机器人行为，大幅缩短开发周期；二是利用合成数据生成技术克服真实数据采集的瓶颈，为模型训练提供多样化的场景；三是开发机器人专属的基础模型，使其具备对物理世界的通用理解能力和技能泛化潜力。

在性能表现上，基于英伟达GPU加速的仿真系统能够实现高保真度的实时物理模拟，而边缘AI平台如Jetson系列则为机器人的实时决策提供了低功耗、高性能的计算支持。与依赖传统编程或小规模数据训练的机器人相比，采用AI学习框架的机器人展现出更强的环境适应性和任务完成鲁棒性。

总体而言，英伟达通过构建从开发、仿真到部署的全栈式物理AI基础设施，正推动机器人技术从限定场景走向开放世界应用。这一技术演进不仅提升了单个机器的智能化水平，更通过云边协同的架构为机器人集群的协同作业奠定了基础，预示着产业自动化将进入一个更灵活、更智能的新阶段。

items.title: Nvidia AI tech claims to slash VRAM usage by 85% with zero quality loss — Neural Texture Compression demo reveals stunning visual parity between 6.5GB of memory and 970MB
items.description: Nvidia has just demoed its Neural Texture Compression technique again at a GTC talk, where it showed VRAM usage dropping from 6.5 GB to just 970 MB in a scene. NTC uses a neural network to decompress textures instead of standard block-based compression, reducing texture size and VRAM usage while also improving final image quality.

You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter As games become more complex and photorealistic, the industry has increasingly relied on upscaling technology to meet surging hardware demands. One of the biggest issues arising from this subpar optimization is VRAM usage, which has risen sharply over the past few years. To combat this, Nvidia has developed a technology called "Neural Texture Compression" (NTC), which was brought up again in today's GTC talk. The best graphics cards will be able to leverage Nvidia's NTC technology.Go deeper with TH Premium: GPUs (Image credit: Noctua)Desktop RoadmapEnterprise RoadmapRubin in-depthThe Stout Owl: The ultimate Noctua G2 PCInstead of conventional block-based compression techniques, NTC enables developers to use small neural networks to unpack textures in any scene. This not only dramatically reduces their size, making game installs more manageable, but also cuts down on VRAM usage at runtime. The resulting textures also look better, with Nvidia claiming up to 4x higher resolution in the final render.In the example below, Nvidia ran a Tuscan Villa Scene that was consuming 6.5 GB of VRAM with standard block compression, but switching to NTC reduced that to just 970 MB, and the image looks identical. Previously, another demo from the company showed a flight helmet with 272 MB of uncompressed textures — block compression cut that down to 98 MB, but NTC reduced it to just 11.37 MB, about 24x less than the original.Article continues below You may like We got a first look at Nvidia's DLSS 5 and the future of neural rendering at GTC Nvidia debuts DLSS 5 for hyper-realistic visual fidelity in games Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang says "the future is neural rendering" at CES 2026 Introduction to Neural Rendering - YouTube Watch On The company also demonstrated Neural Materials, following the same concept: letting a neural network evaluate and decompress material texture data instead of relying on computationally expensive BRDF math. Typically, multiple texture maps are stacked for a material, and the GPU must calculate how light interacts with each layer simultaneously in the rendering pipeline.Neural Materials just asks the neural network how the light will react in that scenario and shades the pixel accordingly. The neural network is trained on all the texture data, so it already knows the result given the light and angle. As such, in the demo scene below, Nvidia achieved up to 7.7x faster render times at 1080p resolution with no loss in image quality.Image 1 of 2(Image credit: Nvidia)(Image credit: Nvidia)NTC is so efficient because it uses matrix acceleration engines, which are a separate hardware block in modern GPUs, so base performance isn't affected. Nvidia calls them Tensor Cores, Intel calls them XMX engines, and AMD calls them AI accelerators. This is where upscalers like DLSS, FSR, and XeSS also live, as they reconstruct a low-res frame into a higher-resolution output, so it's part of Nvidia's neural rendering ambition.The concept of neural rendering doesn't have the widest acclaim in the community yet, and the word "neural network" might lead you to think this is just more AI slop. It's actually the opposite, and one of the better uses of AI since it's not generative at all. NTC will be trained only on the specific set of textures it needs to reference during game development, so there's no chance of hallucination.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsImage 1 of 2(Image credit: Nvidia)(Image credit: Nvidia)Textures by far consume the most VRAM in any game, so any technique to keep them in check is a welcome addition. That said, it's important to note that this isn't exclusive to Nvidia, as Microsoft has standardized it as "Cooperative Vectors" in DirectX. Intel has previously shown off its own demo with noticeably better textures compared to block compression. AMD last talked about the tech in 2024, but it's likely onboard the mission as well.Currently, no game supports Cooperative Vectors or Nvidia's Neural Texture Compression, but we should start to see it implemented soon, given the industry's trajectory. AI has become the answer to seemingly every age-old problem, and corporations are inventing new ways to incorporate it where it doesn't belong. Innovations like NTC, however, show that it can be implemented tastefully to make an actual, meaningful difference. items.author: Hassam Nasir
items.titleZh: 英伟达AI技术宣称无损画质下显存占用锐减85%——神经纹理压缩演示显示6.5GB内存与970MB压缩文件视觉效果毫无差异
items.descriptionZh:
随着游戏内容日趋复杂和逼真，游戏行业愈发依赖超采样等技术来应对激增的硬件性能需求。由此带来的一个突出问题是显存（VRAM）占用的急剧上升。为此，英伟达开发了一项名为“神经纹理压缩”（Neural Texture Compression，简称 NTC）的技术，并在近日的GTC演讲中再次重点提及。

传统的纹理压缩通常采用基于块的压缩技术。与之不同，NTC允许开发者使用小型神经网络在场景中实时解压纹理。这不仅极大地减少了纹理数据的存储大小，使游戏安装包更易管理，还能在游戏运行时显著降低显存占用。同时，经NTC处理后的纹理视觉效果更佳，英伟达声称最终渲染输出的分辨率最高可提升4倍。

在演示中，英伟达运行了一个“托斯卡纳别墅”场景。使用标准块压缩时，该场景消耗6.5 GB显存；而切换到NTC后，显存占用降至仅970 MB，且画质保持完全一致。在另一项旧演示中，一个飞行头盔的未压缩纹理为272 MB，经块压缩后降至98 MB，而NTC则将其压缩到仅11.37 MB，比原始大小减少了约24倍。

英伟达还展示了基于同一理念的“神经材质”技术。该技术让神经网络来评估和解压材质纹理数据，而非依赖计算开销巨大的双向反射分布函数（BRDF）数学计算。通常，一个材质需要堆叠多个纹理贴图，GPU必须在渲染管线中同步计算光线与每一层的交互。“神经材质”则直接询问神经网络在特定光照和角度下会如何反应，并据此对像素进行着色。由于神经网络已预先在全部纹理数据上完成训练，它已“知晓”给定条件下的结果。在演示场景中，英伟达在1080p分辨率下实现了最高7.7倍的渲染速度提升，且画质无损。

NTC的高效性源于其对矩阵加速引擎的利用。矩阵加速引擎是现代GPU中独立的硬件模块（英伟达称为Tensor Core，英特尔称为XMX引擎，AMD称为AI加速器），因此不会影响GPU的基础图形性能。像DLSS、FSR和XeSS这样的超采样技术也运行于此，它们将低分辨率帧重建为高分辨率输出。NTC是英伟达“神经渲染”宏大愿景的一部分。

尽管“神经渲染”概念在社区中尚未获得最广泛的赞誉，且“神经网络”一词容易让人联想到泛滥的生成式AI应用，但NTC技术实则相反，它是AI更具建设性的应用之一，因为它完全不是生成式的。NTC仅在游戏开发阶段，针对其需要调用的特定纹理数据集进行训练，因此不存在产生“幻觉”（即胡编乱造）的可能。

纹理是游戏中消耗显存最多的资源，任何能有效控制其占用的技术都备受欢迎。需要指出的是，这项技术并非英伟达独有。微软已将其标准化为DirectX中的“协作向量”。英特尔此前也展示过自家 demo，其纹理质量明显优于块压缩。AMD在2024年曾讨论过该技术，很可能也在积极推进。

目前，尚未有游戏支持“协作向量”或英伟达的“神经纹理压缩”，但鉴于行业发展趋势，我们应该很快会看到它们被引入实践。虽然AI似乎成为了解决所有老问题的答案，企业也在不断寻找甚至生硬地融入AI的场景，但像NTC这样的创新表明，AI能够以恰当的方式被引入，并产生实际且意义深远的影响。

items.title: Iranian missile blitz takes down AWS data centers in Bahrain and Dubai — Amazon reportedly declares “hard down” status for multiple zones
items.description: Internal company communications indicate that multiple zones at AWS Bahrain and Dubai are "hard down," with no timeline for service restoration.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 5 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. Subscribe to our newsletter Iranian strikes on AWS data centers in Bahrain and Dubai have disrupted services that the company declared multiple zones in the region to have “hard down” status, meaning the affected areas are completely unavailable. According to Big Technology, AWS issued an internal memo stating that operations in the two data centers have been disrupted and that it's working to migrate affected clients' workloads to other regions. The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) has conducted strikes against AWS sites in the Middle East since the start of the war in early March.Go deeper with TH Premium: AI and data centers (Image credit: Microsoft)Photonics and high-speed data movement is the next big AI bottleneckThe data center cooling state of playMassive AI data center buildouts are squeezing energy suppliesUltra Ethernet: The data center interconnection of tomorrow“These two regions continue to be impaired, and services should not expect to be operating with normal levels of redundancy and resiliency,” the internal memo said, according to Big Technology. “We are actively working to free and reserve as much capacity as possible in the region for customers, and services should be scaled to the minimal footprint required to support customer migration.”The AWS sites in the Middle East each have three compute zones, with both data centers reporting “hard down” and “impaired but functioning” zones. More importantly, the company said in its internal communications, “We do not have a timeline for when DXB and BAH will return to normal operations.”Article continues below You may like Iranian drone strikes hit three AWS data centers in the UAE and Bahrain Drones attack several AWS Middle East region data centers amid Iran war, leading to outages AWS Bahrain suffers major disruption due to the ongoing US-Iran conflict Amazon isn’t the only tech company that the ongoing conflict between the United States and Iran has directly hit. The Middle Eastern country has threatened to strike Nvidia, Microsoft, and others as early as the second week of March, after the alleged targeting of a Tehran bank that killed several employees. It has reiterated the threat at the start of April and even struck an Oracle data center later that week.However, while damage to data centers in the Middle East is concerning for the region, the global tech industry has bigger concerns. The regional war has disrupted the flow of oil and its derivatives, especially those that go through the Strait of Hormuz. These include aluminum, helium, and LNG — all of which are crucial in the semiconductor supply chain. And even if the war ends today, the damage to infrastructure could mean it takes months or even years for supplies to return to pre-war levels. items.titleZh: 伊朗导弹闪电式打击致巴林、迪拜AWS数据中心瘫痪——亚马逊确认多区域陷入"彻底瘫痪"状态
items.descriptionZh:
根据汤姆硬件（Tom's Hardware）的报道，近期中东地区的军事冲突已对全球科技基础设施造成直接冲击。伊朗伊斯兰革命卫队（IRGC）自三月初战争爆发以来，持续针对美国科技公司的数据中心发动袭击。最新一轮打击影响了亚马逊云科技（AWS）位于阿联酋迪拜（DXB）和巴林（BAH）的数据中心区域，导致服务严重中断。

**背景与事件详情**
报道称，伊朗的袭击（包括无人机打击）已造成AWS在上述两个地区的多个可用区（AZ）进入“硬性宕机”状态，意味着相关区域的服务完全不可用。根据科技媒体Big Technology获得的一份AWS内部备忘录，该公司确认这两个区域的服务持续“受损”，无法以正常的冗余和弹性水平运行。AWS正在积极行动，试图释放并预留区域内的尽可能多的容量，并将受影响客户的工作负载迁移到其他地区，同时建议服务规模应缩减至支持客户迁移所需的最小范围。

**技术状态与恢复挑战**
AWS在中东的每个区域通常设计有三个计算可用区，以实现高可用性。此次事件中，两个数据中心均报告了“硬性宕机”和“功能受损但仍在运行”的混合状态。最关键的是，AWS在内部沟通中明确指出，**“无法提供迪拜和巴林区域恢复正常运营的时间表”**，这凸显了物理基础设施损坏可能导致的漫长恢复周期。

**冲突的扩大化影响**
亚马逊并非唯一的受害方。早在三月第二周，伊朗就曾威胁将打击英伟达（Nvidia）、微软（Microsoft）等科技巨头，起因是据称针对德黑兰一家银行的袭击导致了人员伤亡。四月初，伊朗重申了这一威胁，并在当周晚些时候袭击了一个甲骨文（Oracle）数据中心。这表明科技基础设施已成为地缘政治冲突中的潜在目标。

**更严峻的全球供应链危机**
然而，对中东数据中心的直接破坏固然令人担忧，但全球科技行业面临着更为深远和严重的威胁。该地区的战争严重扰乱了石油及其衍生品的流通，特别是经由霍尔木兹海峡的运输。这些物资包括**铝、氦和液化天然气（LNG）**——它们都是半导体制造供应链中的关键材料。即使战争立即结束，对港口、运输线路等基础设施的破坏也可能意味着需要数月甚至数年时间，供应才能恢复到战前水平。这种供应链中断将对全球芯片生产，乃至依赖于芯片的各类技术产业（包括AI和数据中心建设）产生级联效应，加剧当前本已紧张的行业瓶颈。

此次事件与汤姆硬件近期关注的系列议题——如AI数据中心瓶颈、冷却技术、超大规模建设对能源的压力以及未来互连技术（如超以太网）——紧密相关，它以一种极端的方式揭示了地缘政治风险如何与前沿科技的发展深度交织，成为影响产业稳定的不可预测变量。

items.title: Microsoft to force updates to Windows 11 25H2 for PCs with older OS versions — 'intelligent' update system uses machine learning to determine when a device is ready
items.description: Windows 11 24H2 systems are being upgraded to 25H2 automatically, as Microsoft aims to streamline updates before support ends.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 9 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. Subscribe to our newsletter Microsoft is force-updating devices running Windows 11 24H2 to the latest 25H2 update. With support for version 24H2 officially ending on October 13, 2026, the move seems to be a part of the company’s effort to keep all devices updated to the latest version of its operating system, at the same time make the entire update experience more streamlined.According to Microsoft’s Windows 11 25H2 support page, the automatic rollout specifically targets systems running Home and Pro editions of Windows 11 version 24H2. However, devices that are managed by organizations or IT departments are excluded at the moment. Notably, the rollout will be handled by an “intelligent” update system that leverages machine learning to determine when a device is ready to receive the update.Curiously, there seems to be a lack of transparency around how Microsoft’s machine learning system decides when a device is ready to receive the automatic update. The company has not shared any specifics about the criteria or any data points that are being used, which does raise some questions, especially for users who prefer greater control, especially at the system-level.Article continues below You may like Microsoft confirms Windows 11 26H1 will be for Arm devices only at launch Microsoft issues emergency update for Windows 11 Some PCs can't boot after latest Windows 11 security update, no fix in sight (Image credit: Microsoft)As this is a forced update, users will not have an option to completely opt out. There is, however, limited control over when the update is installed, as Microsoft will let users postpone for a specific period of time. For those who prefer taking matters into their own hands, the update can also be installed manually by heading to Settings > Windows Update and clicking on “Check for updates,” provided the device meets the eligibility requirements.Since we are on the subject, Microsoft pushed an emergency update for Windows 11 earlier this week following a faulty preview update that failed to install on a large number of systems. The original update (KB5079391) was released in late March, which reportedly triggered widespread installation issues with error code 0x80073712, which typically indicates missing or corrupted files. Microsoft acknowledged the issue and has since pulled the broken update and replaced it with a new out-of-band patch (KB5086672). This updated release not only resolves the installation problems but also includes all the improvements and features originally intended for the March update. items.titleZh: 微软将强制旧版本系统PC升级至Windows 11 25H2——智能更新系统借助机器学习判断设备准备就绪时间
items.descriptionZh:
微软正对运行Windows 11 24H2的设备强制推送最新的25H2版本更新。这一举措的背景是，24H2版本的支持将于2026年10月13日正式终止。微软旨在通过此举使所有设备保持在其操作系统的最新版本，同时使整个更新体验更加 streamlined（顺畅高效）。

根据微软的Windows 11 25H2支持页面，此次自动推送主要针对运行家庭版和专业版Windows 11 24H2的系统。目前，由组织或IT部门管理的设备暂不在此次强制更新范围内。值得注意的是，此次推送将由一个“智能”更新系统处理，该系统利用机器学习来判断设备何时准备好接收更新。

然而，微软的机器学习系统如何决定设备更新就绪时间，目前缺乏透明度。该公司未分享所使用的具体标准或数据点，这引发了一些疑问，特别是对于那些偏好拥有更多控制权（尤其是在系统层面）的用户。

由于这是强制更新，用户将无法选择完全退出。不过，用户在安装时间上拥有有限的控制权，微软允许将更新推迟一段时间。对于喜欢自己掌控过程的用户，如果设备符合条件，也可以手动安装更新：通过进入“设置”>“Windows更新”，然后点击“检查更新”。

谈到更新，本周早些时候，微软还推送了一个Windows 11紧急更新。起因是一个有缺陷的预览更新（KB5079391，发布于3月下旬）在大量系统上安装失败，并报告了错误代码0x80073712（通常表示文件丢失或损坏）。微软已确认该问题，撤回了有问题的更新，并用一个新的带外补丁（KB5086672）取而代之。这个更新版本不仅解决了安装问题，还包含了原定于3月更新中的所有改进和功能。

综上所述，微软正在加强其更新推送策略，利用智能系统强制设备升级以结束支持的服务，但同时其更新机制也面临稳定性和透明度方面的挑战。对于普通用户，这意味着系统将更自动地保持最新，但可能牺牲一定的控制权；对于企业用户，目前仍保留管理自主权。近期紧急修补有缺陷更新的情况，也反映了快速迭代的更新流程仍需在效率与可靠性之间寻求平衡。

items.title: New 'GeForge' and 'GDDRHammer' attacks can fully infiltrate your system through Nvidia's GPU memory — Rowhammer attacks in GPUs force bit flips in protected VRAM regions to gain read/write access
items.description: Two new Rowhammer attacks for GPUs have been discovered that can cause bit flips in VRAM to gain arbitrary read/write access over it. These attacks target page files and the page directory that are otherwise protected from electrical disturbance by the driver. By "massaging" these data structures into vulnerable regions where a bit flip can occur, the attacker can access even the CPU memory.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. Contact me with news and offers from other Future brands Receive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors By submitting your information you agree to the Terms & Conditions and Privacy Policy and are aged 16 or over. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter Last year, we covered the first known Rowhammer attack on GPUs, dubbed "GPUHammer," which proved that VRAM is also susceptible to bit flips. At the time, our story focused on how GPUHammer can cripple an AI model's accuracy from 80% to just 0.1% in an RTX A6000 with a single bit flip. Since then, two new Rowhammer attacks have surfaced that can gain root access to the CPU through your GPU, potentially compromising your entire system (story via Ars Technica).These attacks are called "GDDRHammer" and "GeForge," as listed on the gddr.fail website. They both work quite similarly and share an identical end goal. But first, it's important to understand what Rowhammer is. Back in 2014, it was discovered that by constantly accessing rows of memory, you can induce a bit flip on purpose on neighboring rows. Since bits are stored as electrical charge, "hammering" them causes electrical interference that can flip 0s into 1s, or vice versa.When a bit flips, a bad actor suddenly has a window to access higher privileges in the memory, from where the CPU can be compromised. Rowhammer has always been associated with CPUs because it began on DDR3 RAM and has expanded to more system memory types like DDR5 or DDR4 with ECC. It was only in late 2025 that a GPU was compromised for the first time when researchers attacked GDDR6 VRAM in an RTX A6000. Now, GDDRHammer is taking things one step forward.Article continues below You may like DRAM shortage fuels fake GPU scams as China-based fraudsters exploit the supply crisis Nvidia reportedly working on RTX 5050 with 9GB of VRAM, new RTX 5060 with cut-down GB205 also planned Security researcher says AMD auto-updater downloads software insecurely, enabling remote code execution The concept of forcing a bit flip is the same, but the hammering is smarter now and significantly more effective. Researchers used a technique called "memory massaging" to orchestrate a more controlled attack, which allowed them to push 129 flips per memory bank. It's called massaging because the attack steers the target data structures into memory regions that aren't protected against electrical disturbance (by the GPU drivers).This is done to break the GPU's page tables that point it to physical addresses on the VRAM. Think of it as an app accessing the memory for another app that it has no control over; you're fooling the GPU into breaking its own boundaries. Once the page tables are corrupted and memory isolation is compromised, you gain complete control over the VRAM, allowing both read and write access.GeForge builds upon GDDRHammer but was discovered by a different research team, who went even further and targeted the GPU's page directory instead of page tables. The directory points to these tables, so by inducing bit flips in this data structure, you're compromising the VRAM on an even deeper level. Furthermore, GeForge works even when IOMMU is disabled in the BIOS (more on this later). (Image credit: gddr.fail)The team was able to induce 1,171 bit flips on an RTX 3060 and 202 bit flips on an RTX A6000. That obviously cripples the GPU's memory, but according to the research paper, it also means that "an attacker can modify the page table on the GPU to point to memory on the CPU, thereby giving the attacker the ability to read/write all of the CPU’s memory as well, which of course completely compromises the machine.”Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsSo far, only GDDR6 memory seems to be affected by these Rowhammer attacks, and the team tested 25 GPUs with it, finding vulnerabilities in most. More modern cards with GDDR6X or GDDR7 memory were also tested, but couldn't be compromised yet. Nvidia has already suggested enabling ECC to mitigate against Rowhammer attacks, but that reduces total VRAM capacity, introduces performance overhead, and isn't available on every GPU.The other option is to enable IOMMU (Input–Output Memory Management Unit) in the BIOS, which puts a strict boundary around non-CPU devices when they try to access system memory. Usually, a GPU can read or write system memory without requesting the CPU every time (Direct Memory Access), which is how GDDRHammer or GeForge slip into system RAM. But with IOMMU enabled, corrupted page tables with incorrect mapping couldn't be exploited to gain access to the CPU since they'd be cordoned off.Think of it as putting the GPU in a sandbox where it can still talk to the CPU, but it's not allowed to go around it. Of course, if the IOMMU itself is misconfigured or targeted in an attack, it can be bypassed as well, but it is the best line of defense for now. The researchers themselves admit that enabling IOMMU closes the vulnerability. By default, it's kept disabled in the BIOS to avoid compatibility problems because it's so stringent.Rowhammer attacks, while dangerous, still require the attacker to have access to your system in order to run code that will start the process. So, you're not as actively exposed compared to other remote attacks. However, in a network of shared computers, maybe a cluster of GPUs running AI workloads, the risk is increased, so it's important to stay vigilant and informed, nonetheless. Check out the linked research papers if you want to dive deep into the specifics of how either attack works. items.author: Hassam Nasir
items.titleZh: 新型‘GeForge’与‘GDDRHammer’攻击可通过英伟达GPU内存完全渗透系统——GPU中的Rowhammer攻击强制受保护显存区域发生比特翻转以获取读写权限
items.descriptionZh:
去年，我们报道了首例针对GPU的Rowhammer攻击（名为“GPUHammer”），证实了显存（VRAM）同样存在比特翻转漏洞。当时的研究显示，单个比特翻转就能让RTX A6000上运行的AI模型准确率从80%暴跌至0.1%。此后，又出现了两种新的Rowhammer攻击方式——“GDDRHammer”和“GeForge”（相关漏洞信息发布于gddr.fail网站）。它们不仅能攻击GPU，还能通过GPU获取CPU的根权限，进而威胁整个系统安全。

这两种攻击原理相似且目标一致，但首先需要理解Rowhammer的本质。该漏洞于2014年被发现：通过高频访问内存的特定行，会因电气干扰导致相邻行存储单元发生电荷泄漏，从而引发比特翻转（0变1或1变0）。一旦发生翻转，攻击者便可能利用内存权限漏洞提升访问权限，最终危及CPU。传统Rowhammer攻击主要针对CPU使用的DDR系列内存（如DDR3/DDR4/DDR5），而直到2025年底，研究人员才首次在配备GDDR6显存的RTX A6000上实现了对GPU的Rowhammer攻击。

新一代攻击在核心机制上有了显著进化：
1. **GDDRHammer** 采用了更智能的“内存按摩”技术：通过精确操控数据布局，将攻击目标引导至GPU驱动程序未设防的内存区域，从而实现对内存库更高效的控制（单内存库可引发129次翻转）。该攻击通过破坏GPU页表——即GPU用于映射显存物理地址的关键数据结构——打破内存隔离屏障。这相当于诱骗GPU自行跨越程序间的内存边界，最终使攻击者获得对显存的完全读写控制权。

2. **GeForge** 在GDDRHammer基础上更进一步：它直接针对更底层的GPU页目录发起攻击。页目录是页表的索引，破坏该结构意味着在更深层次上瓦解显存安全。该攻击甚至在BIOS禁用IOMMU（输入输出内存管理单元）时依然有效。实验数据显示，在RTX 3060上可诱发1,171次比特翻转，在RTX A6000上也可实现202次翻转。研究人员指出：“攻击者可通过修改GPU页表，将其指向CPU内存，从而获得对CPU全部内存的读写权限，彻底攻陷整台机器。”

**影响范围与防御措施**：
- 目前确认受影响的显存类型主要为GDDR6，测试涉及的25款GPU大多存在漏洞。更新代的GDDR6X与GDDR7显存尚未被攻破。
- 英伟达建议启用ECC（错误校验纠正）功能缓解攻击，但这会降低可用显存容量、引入性能开销，且并非所有GPU都支持该功能。
- 更有效的防护是启用BIOS中的IOMMU。该单元可为GPU等外设设备设置严格的系统内存访问边界，阻止其通过损坏的页表越界访问CPU内存（相当于将GPU置于“沙箱”中）。但默认情况下IOMMU常处于关闭状态以避免兼容性问题，且若配置不当仍可能被绕过。

**风险场景与局限**：
Rowhammer攻击仍需攻击者先在目标系统上运行恶意代码才能启动攻击过程，因此相较于远程攻击，普通用户面临的风险较低。但在共享计算环境（如运行AI任务的GPU集群）中，风险会显著升高。研究人员强调，启用IOMMU能有效阻断此类漏洞，建议高危环境用户评估开启该功能的必要性。

（注：本文技术细节基于Ars Technica报道及相关学术论文，完整研究可访问gddr.fail获取。）

items.title: Intel's potent 18-core Core Ultra 5 250KF Plus CPU lands at retail for under $200
items.description: Intel's chip matches similarly-priced AMD parts in gaming and mercilessly wallops them in multi-threaded work.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 5 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. Contact me with news and offers from other Future brands Receive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors By submitting your information you agree to the Terms & Conditions and Privacy Policy and are aged 16 or over. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter Casual hardware enthusiasts and folks on the periphery of the hobby may have heard some buzz around Intel's new Arrow Lake Refresh processors, and now the iGPU-less Intel Core Ultra 5 250KF Plus is available for a cool $199, while the full-featured Intel Core Ultra 5 250K Plus is $215. Look, it's not just that the Core Ultra 200S "Plus" processors are that much better than the original recipe (and they are better); rather, it's simply that Arrow Lake was never that bad to begin with. Now that Intel has cut pricing on its processors, the Core Ultra 7 270K Plus and Core Ultra 5 250K Plus are looking downright attractive, especially now that the Core Ultra 5 250K Plus is down to $199 at Newegg.Technically, that's still over the MSR—err, sorry, Recommended Customer Price of $184, but it's the closest any of the new parts have come to their RCP. The buzz around Intel's latest CPUs has been positive enough that demand may have outstripped supply, and so the high-end Core Ultra 7 270K Plus (which is faster than the Core Ultra 9 285K in a lot of situations) is already out of stock in some stores, while the Core Ultra 5 250K Plus is going for some $20 over its suggested price.Intel Core Ultra 5 250KF Plus is $199.99Intel Core Ultra 5 250K Plus is $215So it goes that we have the Core Ultra 5 250KF Plus, which is identical to the 250K, just without the integrated GPU. That's a very slight bummer even for those intending to use powerful discrete gaming GPUs, as it can be handy for troubleshooting (or allowing you to continue using your PC when that big GPU melts its power connector and needs an RMA), but ultimately, the integrated GPU is just inactive silicon in most PC gamers' rigs.AMD does hold the gaming crown in some price bands, but the truth is, there are plenty of PC gamers out there who are all-around power users who make use of their PCs for a lot more than playing video games. You can buy a Core Ultra 5 250KF Plus for $199 and enjoy the multitasking horsepower of eighteen CPU cores cranking at 5.3 GHz, or for the same price, you can pick up a Ryzen 5 9600X, with a whopping six cores. The comparison isn't even salient for multi-core workloads and multi-tasking usage, and in fact, the Intel chip usually wins in gaming against the 9600X anyway.That's right: AMD's Ryzen processors with 3D V-Cache are dominant in gaming workloads, and the Core Ultra 200S Plus series doesn't change that. However, 3D V-Cache processors basically start at $350 with the Ryzen 7 7800X3D outside of some Micro Center doorbusters. That's a brilliant processor, don't get me wrong, and the gaming performance is still a full step ahead of the Intel parts. If you're not gaming in a low resolution (like 1080p) with something like a GeForce RTX 4080 or Radeon RX 9070 XT, though, you'll likely never notice the difference anyway—certainly not the way you'll notice 65% faster render times in HandBrake or 57% faster code compiles.Image 1 of 47(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)There are a few caveats with the newer chips; you'll want the fastest RAM you can afford to throw at the chip (ideally 7200 MT/s or better, although anything will work), and you'll also need to install Intel's Platform Performance Package to get the Application Performance Optimization and Binary Optimization Tool. Fortunately, Intel has made that latter part a one-step process with an all-in-one package instead of requiring users to separately install packages for DTT, PTT, APO, and various other three-letter acronyms.We can't imagine the new Arrow Lake Refresh processors are any less expensive for Intel to fabricate and package, so the company is likely taking a bath on its margins to move these chips. That doesn't matter much to the end-users, though; thanks to Intel's generosity (or desperation), you can pick up a seriously potent 18-core processor for just two-hundred bucks. It's a whole different world from just a few years ago, when Intel's top-end CPU for $539 had a measly eight cores while AMD was shipping 16-core behemoths.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsFollow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
items.author: Zak Killian
items.titleZh: 英特尔劲爆18核酷睿Ultra 5 250KF Plus处理器零售上市，售价不到200美元
items.descriptionZh:
英特尔近期推出的Arrow Lake Refresh处理器系列中，两款新型号——Core Ultra 5 250K Plus和Core Ultra 5 250KF Plus——已正式上市，定价分别为215美元和199美元。此次发布并非因为初代Arrow Lake架构存在显著缺陷，而是英特尔通过价格调整与规格优化，进一步提升了产品的市场竞争力。其中，无集成显卡的250KF Plus版本以199美元的亲民价格，提供了惊人的18核CPU配置，最高频率达5.3 GHz，成为多任务处理与内容创作场景下的高性价比选择。

从技术层面看，Arrow Lake Refresh“Plus”系列主要在原有架构基础上进行了频率优化与软件调校。用户需搭配高速内存（理想速度为7200 MT/s或更高）并安装英特尔统一发布的“平台性能包”，该套件整合了应用性能优化（APO）和二进制优化工具（BOT）等组件，简化了此前需要分步安装多个独立驱动的流程，从而充分发挥处理器性能。

性能对比数据显示，在同价位段中，英特尔Core Ultra 5 250KF Plus（18核）与AMD Ryzen 5 9600X（6核）形成鲜明对比。在多核工作负载如视频渲染（HandBrake）和代码编译中，英特尔处理器领先幅度高达65%和57%。即使在游戏场景中，250KF Plus也普遍优于9600X，尽管AMD的3D V-Cache技术型号（如Ryzen 7 7800X3D）仍在高帧率低分辨率游戏中保持优势，但这些高端型号起价达350美元，与英特尔200美元价位产品不构成直接竞争。

值得注意的是，当前市场对Core Ultra 7 270K Plus等高端型号需求旺盛，部分渠道已出现缺货，而Core Ultra 5 250K Plus的实际售价也略高于184美元的官方建议价。分析认为，英特尔可能为抢占市场份额而主动压缩利润空间，以极具侵略性的定价策略应对AMD在主流市场的竞争。

对于不同用户群体，该系列处理器的价值定位清晰：追求极致游戏帧数且预算充足的玩家仍可能倾向AMD 3D V-Cache产品；但需要兼顾游戏、流媒体、创作等多线程任务的综合型用户，Arrow Lake Refresh处理器凭借翻倍的核心数量与显著的多核性能优势，成为更均衡的选择。无核显的KF版本虽损失了集成显卡的故障排查便利性，但对配备独立显卡的游戏玩家而言，这反而是降低成本的合理选择。

此次价格调整折射出CPU市场竞争的白热化。仅数年前，英特尔539美元的高端产品仅提供8核配置，而如今200美元价位即可获得18核处理器，标志着主流桌面计算平台正式步入“核心战争”新阶段。尽管这可能压缩英特尔短期利润，但最终受益的将是广大消费者，他们能以更低成本获得以往需要高昂投入才能获取的多线程性能。

history.title: A folk musician became a target for AI fakes and a copyright troll
history.description:
Murphy Campbell is at the center of a brewing storm around AI and a broken copyright system. | Image: Murphy Campbell	

In January, folk artist Murphy Campbell discovered several songs on her Spotify profile that did not belong there. They were songs that she had recorded, but she'd never uploaded them to Spotify, and something was off about the vocals.
She quickly surmised that someone had pulled performances of the songs she posted to YouTube, created AI covers, and uploaded them to streaming platforms under her name. I ran one of the songs, "Four Marys", through two different AI detectors, and it seemed to support her suspicions with both saying it was probably AI-generated.
Campbell was shocked, "I was kind of under the impression that we had a little b …
Read the full story at The Verge.
history.content: In January, folk artist Murphy Campbell discovered several songs on her Spotify profile that did not belong there. They were songs that she had recorded, but she’d never uploaded them to Spotify, and something was off about the vocals.She quickly surmised that someone had pulled performances of the songs she posted to YouTube, created AI covers, and uploaded them to streaming platforms under her name. I ran one of the songs, “Four Marys”, through two different AI detectors, and it seemed to support her suspicions with both saying it was probably AI-generated.Campbell was shocked, “I was kind of under the impression that we had a little bit more checks in place before someone could just do that. But, you know, a lesson learned there,” she told The Verge. It took some time before Campbell managed to get the fake songs removed, “I became a pest,” she said. And even then, it wasn’t a complete victory. While the offending tracks don’t appear to be available on YouTube Music or Apple Music anymore, at least one can still be found on Spotify, just under a different artist profile, but with the same name. There are now multiple Murphy Campbells — “Obviously, I was thrilled by that,” the real Murphy Campbell said.Spotify is testing a new system that would allow artists to manually approve songs before they appear on their profile, but Campbell is skeptical after being burned. “I feel like, every time, an entity that’s that large makes a promise like that to musicians. It seems to just not be what they made it out to be, but I’ll be curious to try it out in the future,” she said.This was just the beginning of Campbell’s nightmare, however.RelatedMusicians are getting really tired of this AI clone ‘bullshit’On the day that a Rolling Stone article was published, discussing Campbell’s brush with AI imitators, a series of videos were uploaded to YouTube through distributor Vydia. Those videos have not been posted publicly, and it’s unclear if anyone other than the uploader, who goes by Murphy Rider, has seen them. YouTube declined to comment for this story.Those were used to claim ownership of the material in several of Murphy Campbell’s videos. Campbell received a notice from YouTube reading: “You are now sharing revenues with the copyright owners of the music detected in your video, Darling Corey.” The most confusing part, the songs at the center of these claims are all in the public domain, including the classic “In the Pines,” which dates back to at least the 1870s and has been covered by everyone from Lead Belly to Nirvana (as “Where Did You Sleep Last Night”).Vydia has since released those claims, and spokesperson Roy LaManna says the person who uploaded the videos has been banned from their platform. Of the over 6,000,000 claims filed by Vydia through YouTube’s Content ID system, 0.02 percent were found to be invalid, which LaManna says is, “by industry standards is like amazing.” Continuing, “we pride ourselves on doing this the right way.”LaManna also says that Vydia has no connection to Timeless IR or the AI covers that were uploaded to streaming platforms under Campbell’s name. While the timing is certainly suspicious, LaManna says the two incidents are separate.Vydia has received a lot of blowback including, LaManna says, “literal death threats” which have led to the offices being evacuated. Campbell isn’t about to let Vydia off the hook, but notes that it’s not solely to blame. The worlds of generative AI, music distribution, and copyright are complex with multiple points of failure and opportunities for abuse. “I think it goes way deeper than we think it does,” Campbell says.
history.author: Terrence O’Brien
history.titleZh: 民间音乐家遭AI伪造与版权流氓盯上
history.descriptionZh:
今年1月，民间艺术家墨菲·坎贝尔（Murphy Campbell）在她的Spotify主页上发现了多首不属于自己的歌曲。这些虽是她曾演唱过的作品，却从未亲自上传至该平台，且人声部分存在明显异常。她迅速推断，有人从她发布于YouTube的表演视频中提取音频，通过AI生成仿唱版本，并冒用她的名义上传至流媒体平台。笔者将其一首歌曲《四玛丽》（Four Marys）交由两种不同的AI检测工具分析，结果均显示该曲目很可能由AI生成，印证了她的疑虑。

坎贝尔对此感到震惊：“我原以为平台至少会设置一些审核机制，没想到有人能如此轻易得手。这算是给我上了一课。”她向《The Verge》透露，经过一番周折才成功删除这些伪造歌曲，“我不得不像个讨嫌鬼一样反复投诉”。然而，这场维权并非彻底胜利——虽然涉事曲目已从YouTube Music和Apple Music下架，但至少仍有一首以相同歌名、不同艺术家主页的形式存留在Spotify上。“现在平台上冒出来好几个‘墨菲·坎贝尔’，简直荒唐。”她无奈表示。

尽管Spotify正在测试一项允许艺术家手动审核主页歌曲的新系统，但吃过亏的坎贝尔仍持怀疑态度：“每当这类大型平台向音乐人作出承诺时，结果往往不尽如人意。不过未来我还是会好奇试试看。”可她的噩梦并未就此结束。

在《滚石》杂志报道其遭遇AI仿冒事件的同一天，一批通过分销商Vydia上传的私人视频悄然出现在YouTube后台。这些由名为“墨菲·骑手”（Murphy Rider）的用户上传的视频虽未公开，却触发了YouTube的Content ID系统，导致坎贝尔收到通知：“您的视频《亲爱的科里》（Darling Corey）中检测到受版权保护的音乐，现已与版权方共享收益。”极具讽刺意味的是，被声索版权的歌曲均属公有领域作品，其中包含可追溯至19世纪70年代的经典民谣《在松林中》（In the Pines），该作品曾被从“铅肚皮”（Lead Belly）到涅槃乐队（Nirvana）等无数音乐人演绎。

Vydia随后撤销了这些不当声索，其发言人罗伊·拉曼纳（Roy LaManna）称上传者已被封禁，并强调平台通过Content ID系统提交的600万次版权声明中仅0.02%无效，“按行业标准已属卓越”。但拉曼纳坚称Vydia与涉事AI翻唱及背后公司Timeless IR无关，尽管时间点的巧合令人侧目。

此事引发激烈反弹，拉曼纳透露甚至收到死亡威胁，导致办公室被迫疏散。坎贝尔认为责任并非全在Vydia，生成式AI、音乐分发与版权体系交织成的复杂网络存在多重漏洞与滥用空间。“这潭水比我们想象得更深。”她指出，事件暴露出AI克隆技术滥用、平台审核机制薄弱、版权系统自动化缺陷等多重风险，尤其对独立艺术家的创作生态构成严峻挑战。在技术狂奔与法律滞后的夹缝中，如何构建兼顾创新与权益保护的治理框架，已成为数字音乐时代迫在眉睫的命题。

Advancements in robot learning, simulation and foundation models are accelerating development, enabling robots to move from training in virtual […]
history.content: This National Robotics Week, NVIDIA is highlighting the breakthroughs that are bringing AI into the physical world — as well as the growing wave of robots transforming industries, from agricultural and manufacturing to energy and beyond. Advancements in robot learning, simulation and foundation models are accelerating development, enabling robots to move from training in virtual environments to real-world deployment faster than ever. With NVIDIA platforms for simulation, synthetic data and AI-powered robot learning, developers now have the tools to build machines that can perceive, reason and act in complex environments. Check back here all week for coverage on the latest NVIDIA physical AI technologies.
history.author: NVIDIA Writers
history.titleZh: 全国机器人周——最新实体人工智能研究、突破与资源
history.descriptionZh:
值此国家机器人周之际，英伟达重点展示了将人工智能带入物理世界的突破性进展，以及从农业、制造业到能源等领域日益增长的机器人转型浪潮。机器人学习、仿真和基础模型领域的进步正在加速发展，使得机器人能够比以往任何时候都更快地从虚拟环境训练转向现实世界部署。

借助英伟达在仿真、合成数据和AI驱动的机器人学习方面的平台，开发者如今拥有了构建能够在复杂环境中感知、推理和行动的机器所需的工具。这些技术进步正在深刻改变多个行业：在农业领域，配备了先进视觉系统的机器人可以精准识别并清除杂草，减少除草剂的使用；在制造业，自主移动机器人能够灵活适应动态变化的生产线，提高物流效率；在能源行业，机器人可执行危险环境下的检测和维护任务，保障作业安全。

核心技术原理主要围绕三大支柱：一是通过Isaac Sim等高性能仿真平台，在虚拟世界中生成大量训练数据并安全测试机器人行为，大幅缩短开发周期；二是利用合成数据生成技术克服真实数据采集的瓶颈，为模型训练提供多样化的场景；三是开发机器人专属的基础模型，使其具备对物理世界的通用理解能力和技能泛化潜力。

在性能表现上，基于英伟达GPU加速的仿真系统能够实现高保真度的实时物理模拟，而边缘AI平台如Jetson系列则为机器人的实时决策提供了低功耗、高性能的计算支持。与依赖传统编程或小规模数据训练的机器人相比，采用AI学习框架的机器人展现出更强的环境适应性和任务完成鲁棒性。

总体而言，英伟达通过构建从开发、仿真到部署的全栈式物理AI基础设施，正推动机器人技术从限定场景走向开放世界应用。这一技术演进不仅提升了单个机器的智能化水平，更通过云边协同的架构为机器人集群的协同作业奠定了基础，预示着产业自动化将进入一个更灵活、更智能的新阶段。

history.title: Nvidia AI tech claims to slash VRAM usage by 85% with zero quality loss — Neural Texture Compression demo reveals stunning visual parity between 6.5GB of memory and 970MB
history.description: Nvidia has just demoed its Neural Texture Compression technique again at a GTC talk, where it showed VRAM usage dropping from 6.5 GB to just 970 MB in a scene. NTC uses a neural network to decompress textures instead of standard block-based compression, reducing texture size and VRAM usage while also improving final image quality.
history.content: Share Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. Contact me with news and offers from other Future brands Receive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors By submitting your information you agree to the Terms & Conditions and Privacy Policy and are aged 16 or over. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter As games become more complex and photorealistic, the industry has increasingly relied on upscaling technology to meet surging hardware demands. One of the biggest issues arising from this subpar optimization is VRAM usage, which has risen sharply over the past few years. To combat this, Nvidia has developed a technology called "Neural Texture Compression" (NTC), which was brought up again in today's GTC talk. The best graphics cards will be able to leverage Nvidia's NTC technology.Go deeper with TH Premium: GPUs (Image credit: Noctua)Desktop RoadmapEnterprise RoadmapRubin in-depthThe Stout Owl: The ultimate Noctua G2 PCInstead of conventional block-based compression techniques, NTC enables developers to use small neural networks to unpack textures in any scene. This not only dramatically reduces their size, making game installs more manageable, but also cuts down on VRAM usage at runtime. The resulting textures also look better, with Nvidia claiming up to 4x higher resolution in the final render.In the example below, Nvidia ran a Tuscan Villa Scene that was consuming 6.5 GB of VRAM with standard block compression, but switching to NTC reduced that to just 970 MB, and the image looks identical. Previously, another demo from the company showed a flight helmet with 272 MB of uncompressed textures — block compression cut that down to 98 MB, but NTC reduced it to just 11.37 MB, about 24x less than the original.Article continues below You may like We got a first look at Nvidia's DLSS 5 and the future of neural rendering at GTC Nvidia debuts DLSS 5 for hyper-realistic visual fidelity in games Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang says "the future is neural rendering" at CES 2026 Introduction to Neural Rendering - YouTube Watch On The company also demonstrated Neural Materials, following the same concept: letting a neural network evaluate and decompress material texture data instead of relying on computationally expensive BRDF math. Typically, multiple texture maps are stacked for a material, and the GPU must calculate how light interacts with each layer simultaneously in the rendering pipeline.Neural Materials just asks the neural network how the light will react in that scenario and shades the pixel accordingly. The neural network is trained on all the texture data, so it already knows the result given the light and angle. As such, in the demo scene below, Nvidia achieved up to 7.7x faster render times at 1080p resolution with no loss in image quality.Image 1 of 2(Image credit: Nvidia)(Image credit: Nvidia)NTC is so efficient because it uses matrix acceleration engines, which are a separate hardware block in modern GPUs, so base performance isn't affected. Nvidia calls them Tensor Cores, Intel calls them XMX engines, and AMD calls them AI accelerators. This is where upscalers like DLSS, FSR, and XeSS also live, as they reconstruct a low-res frame into a higher-resolution output, so it's part of Nvidia's neural rendering ambition.The concept of neural rendering doesn't have the widest acclaim in the community yet, and the word "neural network" might lead you to think this is just more AI slop. It's actually the opposite, and one of the better uses of AI since it's not generative at all. NTC will be trained only on the specific set of textures it needs to reference during game development, so there's no chance of hallucination.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsImage 1 of 2(Image credit: Nvidia)(Image credit: Nvidia)Textures by far consume the most VRAM in any game, so any technique to keep them in check is a welcome addition. That said, it's important to note that this isn't exclusive to Nvidia, as Microsoft has standardized it as "Cooperative Vectors" in DirectX. Intel has previously shown off its own demo with noticeably better textures compared to block compression. AMD last talked about the tech in 2024, but it's likely onboard the mission as well.Currently, no game supports Cooperative Vectors or Nvidia's Neural Texture Compression, but we should start to see it implemented soon, given the industry's trajectory. AI has become the answer to seemingly every age-old problem, and corporations are inventing new ways to incorporate it where it doesn't belong. Innovations like NTC, however, show that it can be implemented tastefully to make an actual, meaningful difference. history.author: Hassam Nasir
history.titleZh: 英伟达AI技术宣称无损画质下显存占用锐减85%——神经纹理压缩演示显示6.5GB内存与970MB压缩文件视觉效果毫无差异
history.descriptionZh:
随着游戏内容日趋复杂和逼真，游戏行业愈发依赖超采样等技术来应对激增的硬件性能需求。由此带来的一个突出问题是显存（VRAM）占用的急剧上升。为此，英伟达开发了一项名为“神经纹理压缩”（Neural Texture Compression，简称 NTC）的技术，并在近日的GTC演讲中再次重点提及。

传统的纹理压缩通常采用基于块的压缩技术。与之不同，NTC允许开发者使用小型神经网络在场景中实时解压纹理。这不仅极大地减少了纹理数据的存储大小，使游戏安装包更易管理，还能在游戏运行时显著降低显存占用。同时，经NTC处理后的纹理视觉效果更佳，英伟达声称最终渲染输出的分辨率最高可提升4倍。

在演示中，英伟达运行了一个“托斯卡纳别墅”场景。使用标准块压缩时，该场景消耗6.5 GB显存；而切换到NTC后，显存占用降至仅970 MB，且画质保持完全一致。在另一项旧演示中，一个飞行头盔的未压缩纹理为272 MB，经块压缩后降至98 MB，而NTC则将其压缩到仅11.37 MB，比原始大小减少了约24倍。

英伟达还展示了基于同一理念的“神经材质”技术。该技术让神经网络来评估和解压材质纹理数据，而非依赖计算开销巨大的双向反射分布函数（BRDF）数学计算。通常，一个材质需要堆叠多个纹理贴图，GPU必须在渲染管线中同步计算光线与每一层的交互。“神经材质”则直接询问神经网络在特定光照和角度下会如何反应，并据此对像素进行着色。由于神经网络已预先在全部纹理数据上完成训练，它已“知晓”给定条件下的结果。在演示场景中，英伟达在1080p分辨率下实现了最高7.7倍的渲染速度提升，且画质无损。

NTC的高效性源于其对矩阵加速引擎的利用。矩阵加速引擎是现代GPU中独立的硬件模块（英伟达称为Tensor Core，英特尔称为XMX引擎，AMD称为AI加速器），因此不会影响GPU的基础图形性能。像DLSS、FSR和XeSS这样的超采样技术也运行于此，它们将低分辨率帧重建为高分辨率输出。NTC是英伟达“神经渲染”宏大愿景的一部分。

尽管“神经渲染”概念在社区中尚未获得最广泛的赞誉，且“神经网络”一词容易让人联想到泛滥的生成式AI应用，但NTC技术实则相反，它是AI更具建设性的应用之一，因为它完全不是生成式的。NTC仅在游戏开发阶段，针对其需要调用的特定纹理数据集进行训练，因此不存在产生“幻觉”（即胡编乱造）的可能。

纹理是游戏中消耗显存最多的资源，任何能有效控制其占用的技术都备受欢迎。需要指出的是，这项技术并非英伟达独有。微软已将其标准化为DirectX中的“协作向量”。英特尔此前也展示过自家 demo，其纹理质量明显优于块压缩。AMD在2024年曾讨论过该技术，很可能也在积极推进。

目前，尚未有游戏支持“协作向量”或英伟达的“神经纹理压缩”，但鉴于行业发展趋势，我们应该很快会看到它们被引入实践。虽然AI似乎成为了解决所有老问题的答案，企业也在不断寻找甚至生硬地融入AI的场景，但像NTC这样的创新表明，AI能够以恰当的方式被引入，并产生实际且意义深远的影响。

history.title: Iranian missile blitz takes down AWS data centers in Bahrain and Dubai — Amazon reportedly declares “hard down” status for multiple zones
history.description: Internal company communications indicate that multiple zones at AWS Bahrain and Dubai are "hard down," with no timeline for service restoration.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 5 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter Iranian strikes on AWS data centers in Bahrain and Dubai have disrupted services that the company declared multiple zones in the region to have “hard down” status, meaning the affected areas are completely unavailable. According to Big Technology, AWS issued an internal memo stating that operations in the two data centers have been disrupted and that it's working to migrate affected clients' workloads to other regions. The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) has conducted strikes against AWS sites in the Middle East since the start of the war in early March.Go deeper with TH Premium: AI and data centers (Image credit: Microsoft)Photonics and high-speed data movement is the next big AI bottleneckThe data center cooling state of playMassive AI data center buildouts are squeezing energy suppliesUltra Ethernet: The data center interconnection of tomorrow“These two regions continue to be impaired, and services should not expect to be operating with normal levels of redundancy and resiliency,” the internal memo said, according to Big Technology. “We are actively working to free and reserve as much capacity as possible in the region for customers, and services should be scaled to the minimal footprint required to support customer migration.”The AWS sites in the Middle East each have three compute zones, with both data centers reporting “hard down” and “impaired but functioning” zones. More importantly, the company said in its internal communications, “We do not have a timeline for when DXB and BAH will return to normal operations.”Article continues below You may like Iranian drone strikes hit three AWS data centers in the UAE and Bahrain Drones attack several AWS Middle East region data centers amid Iran war, leading to outages AWS Bahrain suffers major disruption due to the ongoing US-Iran conflict Amazon isn’t the only tech company that the ongoing conflict between the United States and Iran has directly hit. The Middle Eastern country has threatened to strike Nvidia, Microsoft, and others as early as the second week of March, after the alleged targeting of a Tehran bank that killed several employees. It has reiterated the threat at the start of April and even struck an Oracle data center later that week.However, while damage to data centers in the Middle East is concerning for the region, the global tech industry has bigger concerns. The regional war has disrupted the flow of oil and its derivatives, especially those that go through the Strait of Hormuz. These include aluminum, helium, and LNG — all of which are crucial in the semiconductor supply chain. And even if the war ends today, the damage to infrastructure could mean it takes months or even years for supplies to return to pre-war levels. history.titleZh: 伊朗导弹闪电式打击致巴林、迪拜AWS数据中心瘫痪——亚马逊确认多区域陷入"彻底瘫痪"状态
history.descriptionZh:
根据汤姆硬件（Tom's Hardware）的报道，近期中东地区的军事冲突已对全球科技基础设施造成直接冲击。伊朗伊斯兰革命卫队（IRGC）自三月初战争爆发以来，持续针对美国科技公司的数据中心发动袭击。最新一轮打击影响了亚马逊云科技（AWS）位于阿联酋迪拜（DXB）和巴林（BAH）的数据中心区域，导致服务严重中断。

**背景与事件详情**
报道称，伊朗的袭击（包括无人机打击）已造成AWS在上述两个地区的多个可用区（AZ）进入“硬性宕机”状态，意味着相关区域的服务完全不可用。根据科技媒体Big Technology获得的一份AWS内部备忘录，该公司确认这两个区域的服务持续“受损”，无法以正常的冗余和弹性水平运行。AWS正在积极行动，试图释放并预留区域内的尽可能多的容量，并将受影响客户的工作负载迁移到其他地区，同时建议服务规模应缩减至支持客户迁移所需的最小范围。

**技术状态与恢复挑战**
AWS在中东的每个区域通常设计有三个计算可用区，以实现高可用性。此次事件中，两个数据中心均报告了“硬性宕机”和“功能受损但仍在运行”的混合状态。最关键的是，AWS在内部沟通中明确指出，**“无法提供迪拜和巴林区域恢复正常运营的时间表”**，这凸显了物理基础设施损坏可能导致的漫长恢复周期。

**冲突的扩大化影响**
亚马逊并非唯一的受害方。早在三月第二周，伊朗就曾威胁将打击英伟达（Nvidia）、微软（Microsoft）等科技巨头，起因是据称针对德黑兰一家银行的袭击导致了人员伤亡。四月初，伊朗重申了这一威胁，并在当周晚些时候袭击了一个甲骨文（Oracle）数据中心。这表明科技基础设施已成为地缘政治冲突中的潜在目标。

**更严峻的全球供应链危机**
然而，对中东数据中心的直接破坏固然令人担忧，但全球科技行业面临着更为深远和严重的威胁。该地区的战争严重扰乱了石油及其衍生品的流通，特别是经由霍尔木兹海峡的运输。这些物资包括**铝、氦和液化天然气（LNG）**——它们都是半导体制造供应链中的关键材料。即使战争立即结束，对港口、运输线路等基础设施的破坏也可能意味着需要数月甚至数年时间，供应才能恢复到战前水平。这种供应链中断将对全球芯片生产，乃至依赖于芯片的各类技术产业（包括AI和数据中心建设）产生级联效应，加剧当前本已紧张的行业瓶颈。

此次事件与汤姆硬件近期关注的系列议题——如AI数据中心瓶颈、冷却技术、超大规模建设对能源的压力以及未来互连技术（如超以太网）——紧密相关，它以一种极端的方式揭示了地缘政治风险如何与前沿科技的发展深度交织，成为影响产业稳定的不可预测变量。

history.title: Microsoft to force updates to Windows 11 25H2 for PCs with older OS versions — 'intelligent' update system uses machine learning to determine when a device is ready
history.description: Windows 11 24H2 systems are being upgraded to 25H2 automatically, as Microsoft aims to streamline updates before support ends.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 9 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. Contact me with news and offers from other Future brands Receive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors By submitting your information you agree to the Terms & Conditions and Privacy Policy and are aged 16 or over. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter Microsoft is force-updating devices running Windows 11 24H2 to the latest 25H2 update. With support for version 24H2 officially ending on October 13, 2026, the move seems to be a part of the company’s effort to keep all devices updated to the latest version of its operating system, at the same time make the entire update experience more streamlined.According to Microsoft’s Windows 11 25H2 support page, the automatic rollout specifically targets systems running Home and Pro editions of Windows 11 version 24H2. However, devices that are managed by organizations or IT departments are excluded at the moment. Notably, the rollout will be handled by an “intelligent” update system that leverages machine learning to determine when a device is ready to receive the update.Curiously, there seems to be a lack of transparency around how Microsoft’s machine learning system decides when a device is ready to receive the automatic update. The company has not shared any specifics about the criteria or any data points that are being used, which does raise some questions, especially for users who prefer greater control, especially at the system-level.Article continues below You may like Microsoft confirms Windows 11 26H1 will be for Arm devices only at launch Microsoft issues emergency update for Windows 11 Some PCs can't boot after latest Windows 11 security update, no fix in sight (Image credit: Microsoft)As this is a forced update, users will not have an option to completely opt out. There is, however, limited control over when the update is installed, as Microsoft will let users postpone for a specific period of time. For those who prefer taking matters into their own hands, the update can also be installed manually by heading to Settings > Windows Update and clicking on “Check for updates,” provided the device meets the eligibility requirements.Since we are on the subject, Microsoft pushed an emergency update for Windows 11 earlier this week following a faulty preview update that failed to install on a large number of systems. The original update (KB5079391) was released in late March, which reportedly triggered widespread installation issues with error code 0x80073712, which typically indicates missing or corrupted files. Microsoft acknowledged the issue and has since pulled the broken update and replaced it with a new out-of-band patch (KB5086672). This updated release not only resolves the installation problems but also includes all the improvements and features originally intended for the March update. history.titleZh: 微软将强制旧版本系统PC升级至Windows 11 25H2——智能更新系统借助机器学习判断设备准备就绪时间
history.descriptionZh:
微软正对运行Windows 11 24H2的设备强制推送最新的25H2版本更新。这一举措的背景是，24H2版本的支持将于2026年10月13日正式终止。微软旨在通过此举使所有设备保持在其操作系统的最新版本，同时使整个更新体验更加 streamlined（顺畅高效）。

根据微软的Windows 11 25H2支持页面，此次自动推送主要针对运行家庭版和专业版Windows 11 24H2的系统。目前，由组织或IT部门管理的设备暂不在此次强制更新范围内。值得注意的是，此次推送将由一个“智能”更新系统处理，该系统利用机器学习来判断设备何时准备好接收更新。

然而，微软的机器学习系统如何决定设备更新就绪时间，目前缺乏透明度。该公司未分享所使用的具体标准或数据点，这引发了一些疑问，特别是对于那些偏好拥有更多控制权（尤其是在系统层面）的用户。

由于这是强制更新，用户将无法选择完全退出。不过，用户在安装时间上拥有有限的控制权，微软允许将更新推迟一段时间。对于喜欢自己掌控过程的用户，如果设备符合条件，也可以手动安装更新：通过进入“设置”>“Windows更新”，然后点击“检查更新”。

谈到更新，本周早些时候，微软还推送了一个Windows 11紧急更新。起因是一个有缺陷的预览更新（KB5079391，发布于3月下旬）在大量系统上安装失败，并报告了错误代码0x80073712（通常表示文件丢失或损坏）。微软已确认该问题，撤回了有问题的更新，并用一个新的带外补丁（KB5086672）取而代之。这个更新版本不仅解决了安装问题，还包含了原定于3月更新中的所有改进和功能。

综上所述，微软正在加强其更新推送策略，利用智能系统强制设备升级以结束支持的服务，但同时其更新机制也面临稳定性和透明度方面的挑战。对于普通用户，这意味着系统将更自动地保持最新，但可能牺牲一定的控制权；对于企业用户，目前仍保留管理自主权。近期紧急修补有缺陷更新的情况，也反映了快速迭代的更新流程仍需在效率与可靠性之间寻求平衡。

history.title: New 'GeForge' and 'GDDRHammer' attacks can fully infiltrate your system through Nvidia's GPU memory — Rowhammer attacks in GPUs force bit flips in protected VRAM regions to gain read/write access
history.description: Two new Rowhammer attacks for GPUs have been discovered that can cause bit flips in VRAM to gain arbitrary read/write access over it. These attacks target page files and the page directory that are otherwise protected from electrical disturbance by the driver. By "massaging" these data structures into vulnerable regions where a bit flip can occur, the attacker can access even the CPU memory.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. Contact me with news and offers from other Future brands Receive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors By submitting your information you agree to the Terms & Conditions and Privacy Policy and are aged 16 or over. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter Last year, we covered the first known Rowhammer attack on GPUs, dubbed "GPUHammer," which proved that VRAM is also susceptible to bit flips. At the time, our story focused on how GPUHammer can cripple an AI model's accuracy from 80% to just 0.1% in an RTX A6000 with a single bit flip. Since then, two new Rowhammer attacks have surfaced that can gain root access to the CPU through your GPU, potentially compromising your entire system (story via Ars Technica).These attacks are called "GDDRHammer" and "GeForge," as listed on the gddr.fail website. They both work quite similarly and share an identical end goal. But first, it's important to understand what Rowhammer is. Back in 2014, it was discovered that by constantly accessing rows of memory, you can induce a bit flip on purpose on neighboring rows. Since bits are stored as electrical charge, "hammering" them causes electrical interference that can flip 0s into 1s, or vice versa.When a bit flips, a bad actor suddenly has a window to access higher privileges in the memory, from where the CPU can be compromised. Rowhammer has always been associated with CPUs because it began on DDR3 RAM and has expanded to more system memory types like DDR5 or DDR4 with ECC. It was only in late 2025 that a GPU was compromised for the first time when researchers attacked GDDR6 VRAM in an RTX A6000. Now, GDDRHammer is taking things one step forward.Article continues below You may like DRAM shortage fuels fake GPU scams as China-based fraudsters exploit the supply crisis Nvidia reportedly working on RTX 5050 with 9GB of VRAM, new RTX 5060 with cut-down GB205 also planned Security researcher says AMD auto-updater downloads software insecurely, enabling remote code execution The concept of forcing a bit flip is the same, but the hammering is smarter now and significantly more effective. Researchers used a technique called "memory massaging" to orchestrate a more controlled attack, which allowed them to push 129 flips per memory bank. It's called massaging because the attack steers the target data structures into memory regions that aren't protected against electrical disturbance (by the GPU drivers).This is done to break the GPU's page tables that point it to physical addresses on the VRAM. Think of it as an app accessing the memory for another app that it has no control over; you're fooling the GPU into breaking its own boundaries. Once the page tables are corrupted and memory isolation is compromised, you gain complete control over the VRAM, allowing both read and write access.GeForge builds upon GDDRHammer but was discovered by a different research team, who went even further and targeted the GPU's page directory instead of page tables. The directory points to these tables, so by inducing bit flips in this data structure, you're compromising the VRAM on an even deeper level. Furthermore, GeForge works even when IOMMU is disabled in the BIOS (more on this later). (Image credit: gddr.fail)The team was able to induce 1,171 bit flips on an RTX 3060 and 202 bit flips on an RTX A6000. That obviously cripples the GPU's memory, but according to the research paper, it also means that "an attacker can modify the page table on the GPU to point to memory on the CPU, thereby giving the attacker the ability to read/write all of the CPU’s memory as well, which of course completely compromises the machine.”Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsSo far, only GDDR6 memory seems to be affected by these Rowhammer attacks, and the team tested 25 GPUs with it, finding vulnerabilities in most. More modern cards with GDDR6X or GDDR7 memory were also tested, but couldn't be compromised yet. Nvidia has already suggested enabling ECC to mitigate against Rowhammer attacks, but that reduces total VRAM capacity, introduces performance overhead, and isn't available on every GPU.The other option is to enable IOMMU (Input–Output Memory Management Unit) in the BIOS, which puts a strict boundary around non-CPU devices when they try to access system memory. Usually, a GPU can read or write system memory without requesting the CPU every time (Direct Memory Access), which is how GDDRHammer or GeForge slip into system RAM. But with IOMMU enabled, corrupted page tables with incorrect mapping couldn't be exploited to gain access to the CPU since they'd be cordoned off.Think of it as putting the GPU in a sandbox where it can still talk to the CPU, but it's not allowed to go around it. Of course, if the IOMMU itself is misconfigured or targeted in an attack, it can be bypassed as well, but it is the best line of defense for now. The researchers themselves admit that enabling IOMMU closes the vulnerability. By default, it's kept disabled in the BIOS to avoid compatibility problems because it's so stringent.Rowhammer attacks, while dangerous, still require the attacker to have access to your system in order to run code that will start the process. So, you're not as actively exposed compared to other remote attacks. However, in a network of shared computers, maybe a cluster of GPUs running AI workloads, the risk is increased, so it's important to stay vigilant and informed, nonetheless. Check out the linked research papers if you want to dive deep into the specifics of how either attack works.
